channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Flexible active-matrix micro-LED display with 1T-1FeMFET architecture featuring scaling-limit-free design
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71182-9

Flexible displays face challenges in power use, resolution, and durability. This work presents a flexible micro-LED display driven by HZO-based FeMFETs, achieving low power, high resolution, and strong mechanical reliability.
item.dc:title: Flexible active-matrix micro-LED display with 1T-1FeMFET architecture featuring scaling-limit-free design
item.dc:creator: Tingrui Huang
Guangan Yang
Yimeng Sang
Fulin Zhuo
Mingming Liu
Gengyu Li
Heng Cheng
Zuoxu Yu
Zhiyuan Fu
Siyang Liu
Haoliang Shen
Zhihao Yu
Bin Liu
Xinran Wang
Wangran Wu
Runxiao Shi
Zhe Zhuang
Weifeng Sun

item.title: Accelerated directional growth of seaweed-like iron oxide branches driven by localized electric fields of gold nanoparticles in liquid
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71352-9

Controlling the morphology of dendritic nanostructures is crucial for enhancing their functionality, but remains a challenge. Here, the authors demonstrate that the local electric field between gold nanoparticles and iron oxide branches promotes directional and accelerated growth.
item.dc:title: Accelerated directional growth of seaweed-like iron oxide branches driven by localized electric fields of gold nanoparticles in liquid
item.dc:creator: Mingrui Zhou
Wen Wang
Jinyi Sun
Yuze Yu
Meng Nie
Hubiao Huang
Haimei Zheng
Tao Xu
Litao Sun

item.title: Emergent surface magnetic ordering and surface electronic dipole layers in a two-dimensional spin=1/2 La<sub>2</sub>CuO<sub>4</sub> film
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69457-2

Disentangling the exotic phases that can emerge at the surfaces of strongly correlated materials from the bulk is experimentally difficult. Here, Jain, Diao, Ong, Rusydi, and coauthors succeed in doing so, using grazing incident-angle dependent resonant X-ray scattering to find an emergent surface magnetic ordering and surface electronic dipole layers in the two-dimensional film of La2CuO4
item.dc:title: Emergent surface magnetic ordering and surface electronic dipole layers in a two-dimensional spin=1/2 La<sub>2</sub>CuO<sub>4</sub> film
item.dc:creator: Anjali Jain
Caozheng Diao
Bin Leong Ong
Angga Dito Fauzi
Kaushik Jayaraman
Muhammad Avicenna Naradipa
Sai Prashanth Josyula
Xiao Chi
Thomas James Whitcher
Muhandis Shiddiq
Mark B. H. Breese
Eng Soon Tok
Andrivo Rusydi
